lifecycle_order.label: create lane
lifecycle_order.description: Lane is created via Secretary.createLane() or equivalent — persisted as a durable event

lifecycle_order.label: binder exists immediately
lifecycle_order.description: After lane creation, binder state exists and is reconstructable

lifecycle_order.label: scout completes
lifecycle_order.description: Cartographer/scout role produces scout report; results are persisted via handleRoleOutput

lifecycle_order.label: scope is synthesized
lifecycle_order.description: Scope is synthesized from scout output and persisted

lifecycle_order.label: architect produces plan
lifecycle_order.description: Architect role produces an architecture plan; persisted via handleRoleOutput

lifecycle_order.label: critic approves or rejects
lifecycle_order.description: Critic role reviews the plan; approval advances to execution, rejection routes to repair

lifecycle_order.label: claims are acquired before execution
lifecycle_order.description: Claims must be acquired (or explicitly waived) before executor can apply material work

lifecycle_order.label: executor applies material work
lifecycle_order.description: Surgeon/executor role applies edits; file.edited and edit.applied events are persisted

lifecycle_order.label: validator validates after latest edit
lifecycle_order.description: Validation must be proven to happen after the latest material edit

lifecycle_order.label: red team records findings
lifecycle_order.description: Red team runs adversarial tests; findings (blocking and non-blocking) are persisted

lifecycle_order.label: blocking findings route to repair
lifecycle_order.description: Blocking findings trigger architect→critic→surgeon→trial repair cycle

lifecycle_order.label: non-blocking findings are preserved
lifecycle_order.description: Non-blocking findings are recorded but do not force repair; lane proceeds

lifecycle_order.label: historian creates checkpoint
lifecycle_order.description: Checkpoint is created via CheckpointCreated event

lifecycle_order.label: lane is returned
lifecycle_order.description: LaneReturned event emitted; lane enters terminal state

lifecycle_order.label: binder finalizes after required evidence
lifecycle_order.description: Binder.finalized is the final mutation; no evidence added after

lifecycle_order.label: terminal lane rejects later mutation
lifecycle_order.description: After returned, any further mutation or role output produces typed terminal-lane error; binder digest does not change

lifecycle_order.label: loadLane reconstructs from durable events
lifecycle_order.description: Cold-start reconstruction: loadLane rebuilds state from durable events without relying on in-memory cache

critical_invariants: Cold-start reconstruction must use same PGlite/EventStore data while recreating Secretary

Binder evidence must contain actual sections: scout report, architecture plan, critic review, execution events, validation results, red-team findings, checkpoint, handoff, terminal status, artifact digest
binder.finalized must be the final binder mutation event — no evidence added after finalization
After returned, any further mutation or role output must produce a typed terminal-lane error — binder digest must not change
An approved lane must only advance to executing after positive claim evidence exists; no-claim path must be explicitly represented
Validation must be proven to happen after the latest material edit (including both file.edited and edit.applied)
Blocking findings → repair. Non-blocking findings → recorded but lane proceeds if RedTeamCompleted says zero blocking findings
